pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce Zod as a TypeScript-first schema validation library, covering its main features, design philosophy, and core capabilities

pages.title: Installation and Setup
pages.purpose: Guide users through installing Zod and basic setup for different environments (Node.js, Deno, browsers)

pages.title: Quick Start
pages.purpose: Provide immediate examples of basic schema definition and validation to get users started quickly

pages.title: Core Concepts
pages.purpose: Explain fundamental concepts like schema definition, validation pipeline, type inference, and the relationship between runtime and compile-time types etc.

pages.title: Schema Definition
pages.purpose: Deep dive into how schemas are created and the ZodType base class architecture

pages.title: Validation Pipeline
pages.purpose: Explain the internal validation flow, ParseContext, ParseStatus, and how data flows through the system

pages.title: Schema Types
pages.purpose: Comprehensive reference for all schema types available in Zod

pages.title: Primitive Types
pages.purpose: Cover string, number, boolean, date, bigint, and other primitive schema types with their validation methods

pages.title: Object Schemas
pages.purpose: Detailed coverage of object validation, including shape definition, key handling, and object manipulation methods

pages.title: Array and Tuple Schemas
pages.purpose: Cover array validation, tuple definitions, length constraints, and element validation

pages.title: Union and Intersection
pages.purpose: Explain union types (OR logic) and intersection types (AND logic) with discriminated unions

pages.title: Record and Collection Types
pages.purpose: Cover Record, Map, Set schemas and validation of key-value collections

pages.title: Enum and Literal Types
pages.purpose: Cover enum schemas, literal values, and native TypeScript enum integration

pages.title: Special Types
pages.purpose: Cover function schemas, promise validation, instanceof checks, and custom schema types

pages.title: Validation and Parsing
pages.purpose: Comprehensive guide to parsing methods, validation options, and data processing

pages.title: Parse Methods
pages.purpose: Detail the differences between .parse(), .safeParse(), and their async variants

pages.title: Async Validation
pages.purpose: Cover asynchronous parsing, async refinements, and promise-based validation

pages.title: Schema Transformations
pages.purpose: Explain .transform(), .default(), and data transformation capabilities

pages.title: Custom Refinements
pages.purpose: Cover .refine() and .superRefine() for custom validation logic

pages.title: Schema Composition
pages.purpose: Cover advanced schema manipulation like .partial(), .pick(), .omit(), .extend()

pages.title: Error Handling
pages.purpose: Complete guide to Zod's error system, error formatting, and custom error messages

pages.title: ZodError Structure
pages.purpose: Explain the ZodError class, ZodIssue types, and error categorization

pages.title: Error Formatting
pages.purpose: Cover .format(), .flatten(), and different ways to present validation errors

pages.title: Custom Error Messages
pages.purpose: Explain error maps, custom error messages, and internationalization support

pages.title: Advanced Usage
pages.purpose: Advanced patterns, recursive types, utility functions, and complex schema scenarios

pages.title: Recursive Types
pages.purpose: Cover z.lazy() and handling self-referencing schemas

pages.title: Utility Types and Helpers
pages.purpose: Cover z.infer, utility functions, and type manipulation helpers

pages.title: Development
pages.purpose: Guide for contributors and information about the development process

pages.title: Contributing
pages.purpose: Instructions for contributing to Zod, development setup, and coding standards

pages.title: Build System
pages.purpose: Explain the multi-platform build process, package configuration, and distribution

pages.title: Testing
pages.purpose: Overview of the test suite, cross-platform testing, and test organization

pages.title: Architecture
pages.purpose: Deep dive into Zod's internal architecture, design decisions, and implementation details

pages.title: Type System Design
pages.purpose: Explain the ZodType hierarchy, type inference mechanism, and internal type structures

pages.title: Multi-Platform Support
pages.purpose: Explain how Zod supports Node.js, Deno, and browsers with shared codebase

pages.title: Documentation Website
pages.purpose: Information about the Zod documentation site, blog, and community resources
